judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with nearly identical substance and citations; they are comparable in completeness, though Report 1 additionally flags the doc's payment-integration claim more explicitly as a third discrepancy while Report 2 folds it in similarly.

report_1.evidence: New refund calls should live in `medibill/backend/app/billing/gateway.py`... a full `grep -rn "chargekit"` over medibill shows exactly two violations: `backend/app/api/invoices.py:4,14-16` ... `backend/app/workers/dunning.py:2,9` ... errors.py (imports only error classes, `errors.py:6-9`).

report_2.evidence: New provider calls belong in `medibill/backend/app/billing/gateway.py`... `medibill/backend/app/api/invoices.py:14-16` ... `medibill/backend/app/workers/dunning.py:9-13` ... Those are the only two; `gateway.py` and `errors.py` are the remaining `chargekit` importers and are both legitimate.

report_1.evidence: claims the response contains "exactly the fields: id, tenant_id, amount_cents, currency, status". False — `InvoiceOut` in `backend/app/models/invoice.py:16-22` includes a sixth field, `external_ref`... Migration `backend/migrations/0006_add_invoice_external_ref.sql` shows this field was added later

report_2.evidence: In reality `InvoiceOut` (`backend/app/models/invoice.py:16-22`), which is what the charge endpoint returns (`invoices.py:18`), also carries a sixth field, `external_ref`, added along with migration `backend/migrations/0006_add_invoice_external_ref.sql`

report_1.evidence: The doc should be corrected before or with the first refund commit, and the correction must land in the same commit/PR as any code it describes changing — drift is a bug, not deferred housekeeping.

report_2.evidence: It should be updated as part of the refund feature's first PR — before or alongside the new code, not after — because the refund work will be designed against this document

report_1.evidence: the code handles 5, including `payment_pending`... I would change *how* it's stated: drop the hardcoded count entirely. A literal number is guaranteed to drift the moment a handler is added

report_2.evidence: The handler at `backend/app/api/webhooks.py:10-19` actually handles **5**... an exact hardcoded enumeration in prose is guaranteed to drift every time a case is added. I would either point readers at `app/api/webhooks.py` as the source of truth
